name: Code Upgrader Playground
summary: The inverse of Module-as-a-Service: take EXISTING legacy code and lift it onto the standard. Each card runs the shared engine — legacy source → neutral structure (IR) → generated <component> → verify gate → live element. The AI analyzer is a swappable provider (MVP ships a deterministic reference one, no key); output is only offered when it re-parses, round-trips without drift, and references real intents. Backlog #094.
description:
Concept

A live surface for backlog #094 — an AI upgrader that takes existing/legacy code and lifts it onto the standard. It is the inverse of Module-as-a-Service: MaaS serves one authored component in many forms; the upgrader produces the authored <component> from a legacy component in the first place. Each card shows the whole pipeline: legacy source → neutral structure (IR) → generated <component> → verify gate → live element.

AI is a swappable provider

The analyzer (code → IR) is registry-backed — the same inject-a-provider shape as the MaaS compiler seam. This MVP injects a deterministic reference provider (no model key) that handles one source path (vanilla web components); a bring-your-own-key model provider drops in for messier input with no other change.

The verify gate is the moat

Output is only offered when it re-parses, round-trips to the analyzed structure without drift, and references only real intents. Generic AI emits plausible code; this emits checked code. Un-verified output is shown for inspection but never presented as trusted — and the generated <component> feeds the same MaaS serve() core, so it is a front door onto the standard, not a parallel generator.
